feed.title: SAP Community - SAP NetWeaver
feed.subtitle: SAP NetWeaver Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP GRC NFE NT 2024 001 (CTe 4.00)
entry.content: Poderia nos ajudar a entender quando será lançado alguma SAP Note referente a NT 2024 001 (CTe 4.00)

entry.title: Previous data showing in SAP ui5 screen
entry.content:
1. open a first page with navigation list of 2 line items
  2. Navigate to 2nd page using first line item
  3. Go back and opne 2nd line item
  4. busy dialog open along with first line item's data

when we open second line item, previous data is showing along with busy dialong.

Please help how to clear previous data while current page busy dialouge shows.

entry.title: Link for download media 51055918 for SQL server 2022
entry.content:
We are looking for media 51055918 for SQL server 2022

SQL Server RDBMS medium (for runtime customers)

entry.title: Declustering
entry.content:
Hello, I have a question about the declustering of the payroll and time data. I have found quite a few bits of information that are stated as fact, but are confusing and in the case of some of them, seem to be contradictory:

Declustering is mandatory and automatically performed when the Hana database is used.
Declustering is mandatory and automatically performed when you run Netweaver 7.5 or above.
There is a switch that activates declustering in the SAP system.
When declustering is active, payroll results are still written to the cluster tables and transferred to the declustered tables as part of the payroll process.

In my case, we're running on NetWeaver 7.5 with the Hana database, but when I check some of the declustered tables, such as p2rx_rt, they exist, but appear to be empty. How does declustering work? Does it have to be activated manually? If so, why is it said to be mandatory for NetWeaver 7.5 and/or Hana? The only thing that appears to have happened in my case, is that the declustered tables were created, which doesn't seem like much of a difference.

entry.title: DBA Cockpit Webdynpro not displaying graphical explain
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I am currently trying to analyse expensive sql statements in the DBACOCKPIT but when trying to use the Webdynpro DBACOCKPIT Explain, the screen stays empty:

I activated all SICF Nodes in according to this node:

1245200 - DBA: ICF Service Activation for WebDynpro DBA Cockpit 

I also regenerated the views via the transaction SGEN. Ran all the connection test without any issues.

I checked ST22 and SU53 but see no dumps or authorization errors.

In another thread it was mention that the hostname could be an issue but this is working fine. The only thing I saw was that I receive an error in the web browser console for a dependency not being found, but checking SICF I couldn't find any missing nodes.

Does anyone has a clue or did I miss anything for the configuration?

Thanks for your effort and time.

BR,

Andreas

entry.title: SQL5137N error during System Rename
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We are using DB2 on windows and doing System Rename. At the end of the stage we receive the error

I look at sapinst_dev and managed to get the root cause:

update dbm cfg using DIAGPATH \\ATSUGA\db2dumpBWD
SQL5137N The entry in the database manager configuration file for the
diagnostic directory path (diagpath) is invalid.

Please suggest to me how to resolve this error

Thank you

ZULFADZLY IQBAL

entry.title: IDoc DEBMAS07 - how to update KNVV-LIFSD (delivery block) with blank value
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm able to update KNVV-LIFSD of customer accounts with valid values, for example XX in IDoc DEBMAS under segment E1KNA1M/E1KNVVM.

Then I want to clear the delivery block for some accounts. So what I do is I populate the field LIFSD as blank under E1KNA1M/E1KNVVM. However, it gives me the error "Field KNVV-LIFSD . is not an input field"

Does anyone know how to clear KNVV-LIFSD with IDoc?

Regards,

Minh

entry.title: Homologar o CTe 4.0 junto a SEF-MG.
entry.content:
Estamos homologando o CTE 4.0 junto a SEF MG e estamos recebendo o erro 599 ao emitir um CTe, apesar do xml passar nos validadores. 

O erro é semelhante ao reportado em 12802345  mas como ainda não temos o CTe 4.0 em produção estou passando por isso apenas no ambiente de homologação.  Alguém está usando o ambiente de homologação da SEF-MG para emissão de CTe poderia me confirmar se passa pelo mesmo problema?

Utilizamos o SLL-NFE, release 900 SP 0035

entry.title: Oracle linux/OS upgrade
entry.content:
Hello team,

ALL our sap systems are on AWS with oracle database and oracle linux 7.x. since support is getting expired this year end, we would like to have some recommendations from you; 

  1. what is the best/recommended approach for upgrade --> in-place or out-of-place/fresh install
  2. Any SAP notes/documentation referring to oracle linux upgrade?
  3. Do you recommend any technical procedure for the same. 

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Verification of information about users.
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We need to extract information about whether users were created, changed or deleted in the databases of SAP environments.
How is it possible to extract this information from the database?

Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: ASCS pse certificates expiration monitoring
entry.content:
Good day,
We use standard system monitoring via SM7.2.
We have some problems with monitoring SSL certificates expiration in our NW systems.
Monitoring works fine for ABAP application instance (STRUST), but it is not monitoring ASCS (message server) pse certificates expiration.
Our ASCS (Message server) instance on the separate server, so it has own SSL certificates (server, client). Monitoring does not see it.
Could you help us to set up monitoring for ASCS pse certificates? 
I investigated other templates, but didn't find any appropriate.

PS: In most cases ASCS uses DI certificates via symlink, but in our situation it is not possible (cluster landscape, DI separate server).
PPS: ASCS does not have WD.

entry.title: Verwendung des BRTOOLS für Shrinken einer LOB Tabelle vor der Reorg.
entry.content:
Für die Reorganisierung der Tabelle muss vorerst die verknüpfte LOB Tabelle über einen SHRINK Befehl verkleinert werden.

Im normalen DBA Umfeld wäre es dieses Kommando:

ALTER TABLE <table> MODIFY LOB(DATEN) (SHRINK SPACE);

 

Bei SAP Datenbanken soll das BRTOOLS verwendet werden. Die Frage ist, wie das richtig Vorgehen ist, um die LOB Tabelle zu shrinken?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to convert PDF to PDF/A by using program FP_PDF_TEST_33
entry.content:
There has a requirement to convert the PDF file to PDF/A.

And how to convert it by using program FP_PDF_TEST_33.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP Background Job Processing
entry.content:
Introduction:

In this blog post I am going to explain the steps required for Background Job processing.

What are Background Jobs:

Background jobs are automated tasks that run in the background without any user interaction. They are scheduled to run at specific times and are useful for executing time-consuming and repetitive tasks such as data archiving, batch processing, and report generation. In SAP, background jobs are scheduled using the Background Job Scheduler.

T Codes for Background Processing:

SE38 – To Create variant for background job.

SM36 – To schedule the job.

SM37 – To Monitor the job.

Step-by-Step Guide for Scheduling a Background Job in SAP:

Step 1: Create a Variant to schedule the background job: -

Before you can create a background job, you need to define the variant for the job. A variant is a set of parameters that define how the job is executed. To create a variant, follow the below steps.

Execute SE38 transaction code and enter the name of the program you want to execute in the Program field and select Variants.

Execute it to create a new variant as per your requirement. Select and enter all the required parameters and click on save as variant.

In the next screen enter the name of the variant and description and save the variant.

Step 2: Schedule the background job:

Execute SM36 or You can go to transaction SM36 from SAP Easy Access menu. SAP Menu -> Tools -> CCMS -> Background Processing -> SM36

Enter the name of the background job in the Job Name field and click on the Step button to add a step to the job.

Select the Program of the job you want execute and select its variant in the variant field which you have created and enter the user with which it should be run. Click on the save button.

You can create any number of steps as per your requirement by clicking on the below create button.

Once the step got saved go back and click on the Start condition.

Click on Date/Time and enter the date to which it should be scheduled and the time at which it should be run. Also, you can select the frequency of the job as per your requirement by clicking on the period and save.

If you click on the Restrictions, you can see few more restrictions and that’s depends on your requirement of the job. Select and save.

Go back to the initial screen and click on save. Now the background job got released to run.

You can click on E-Mail Notification and give the mail IDs to whom the status of the job can be sent.

And Spool list recipient is for the people to whom the output report should be send.

Step 3: Monitor the background job: -

Execute SM37 to check the status of the released background job and enter the name of the batch job which you have scheduled in Job name field and select the parameters and click on execute.

You can see the status of the batch job after executing.

You can also edit or suspend or copy and schedule one more job if needed by clicking on Job button.

You can see the job log if the background job got failed or completed successfully. Click on the Job log button.

Conclusion: -

In conclusion, creating background jobs in SAP is a way to automate time-consuming and repetitive tasks, freeing up resources for other important tasks.

entry.title: More than one consumption in reprocess - K265 Register
entry.content:
K265 register
Hello.
I need support to understand how to generate the K265 reprocessing record when consumption of material equal to that produced and also other materials occurs.
I was unable to understand the composition of the shadow /TMF/D_REPR_MAT for these cases.

Example:
Material produced = mat1
Material consumed = mat1
Material consumed = mat2
Material consumed = mat3

entry.title: TCP/IP: setting keepalive interval for Oracle Solaris
entry.content:
Could you tell me how to set "keepalive interval" in Solaris operating system?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Taking huge heap memory by SAP JCO library
entry.content:
we are seeing java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space in SAP Java connector. After analyzing heap dumps, we came to know is, for 278 MB file, jco is taking around 2GB heap memory. 

For 400 mb file, it taking around 3.2GB.

1. We need to know if the Idoc file is having x size , how much approximate memory required (2x, 4x,6x,8x,10x) to process this file using java program.

For example, if the Idoc file is 100 MB, do we need around 400/600/800 MB of java heap  memory to process the file.

2. Is there anyway by which java application will get to know the file size using SapJCO api before start processing/reading file?

Error:

One instance of “com.sap.conn.rfc.data.ARFCSDATA” loaded by “com.test.$Loader @ 0x60129d7a8” occupies 2,790.48 MB (34.17%) bytes. The memory is accumulated in one instance of “char[][]”, loaded by “<system class loader>”, which occupies 2,790.47 MB (34.17%) bytes.

entry.title: How to check the socket communication log
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Check the connection status between SAP and external devices at the socket communication handshake level.
Could you tell me how to check the logs (transactions)?
For example:
socket->bind()->listen()->connect()->accept()
at the log level.
If available, the transaction code and the log name to be referenced
I would appreciate it if you could tell me.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to check Netweaver version on SAP Java system ?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am contacting you because I'd like to check the Netweaver version of my Java system. Can you tell me what steps I need to take?

 

Thank in advance.

Regards,

entry.title: production orders at work center using cois_select_order_data_read
entry.content: I need to pull production orders at a specific workcenter, but I cannot seem to find any success using the cois_select_order_data_read fm. Any suggestions?
